steps.canonical_text: Ask if they are choking. If they can cough or speak, encourage them to keep coughing forcefully.
If they cannot cough, speak, or breathe, lean them forward and give up to 5 back blows firmly between the shoulder blades with the heel of your hand.

asset_refs.caption: Give firm back blows between the shoulder blades
asset_refs.content_description: A hand positioned to deliver a back blow between the shoulder blades.

steps.canonical_text: If the object is not out, stand behind them, place a fist just above the navel, and give up to 5 abdominal thrusts inward and upward. If they are pregnant or markedly obese, use chest thrusts instead of abdominal thrusts.

asset_refs.caption: Place the fist just above the navel for abdominal thrusts
asset_refs.content_description: A close-up showing fist placement just above the navel for an abdominal thrust.

asset_refs.caption: Stand behind the person and deliver abdominal thrusts inward and upward
asset_refs.content_description: A rescuer standing behind a choking adult to deliver abdominal thrusts.

steps.canonical_text: Keep alternating 5 back blows and 5 abdominal thrusts until the object comes out, they can breathe, or they become unresponsive.
If they become unresponsive, call emergency services now, lower them to the floor, and start chest compressions.

asset_refs.caption: If the person becomes unresponsive, lower them to the floor and start chest compressions
asset_refs.content_description: A rescuer performing chest compressions on an unresponsive choking adult.
